diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

diagnostics.message: "scroll" listener without { passive: true } — blocks scrolling performance. Only add { passive: true } if the handler does NOT call event.preventDefault() (passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault())
diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler does NOT call `event.preventDefault()` — passive listeners silently ignore `preventDefault()`, which breaks features like pull-to-refresh suppression, custom gestures, and nested-scroll containment.

A form label must be associated with a control.
diagnostics.help: Either give the label a `htmlFor` attribute with the id of the associated control, or wrap the label around the control.

diagnostics.message: A form label must be associated with a control.
diagnostics.help: Either give the label a `htmlFor` attribute with the id of the associated control, or wrap the label around the control.

diagnostics.message: Non-descriptive handler name "handleChange" — name should describe what it does, not when it runs
diagnostics.help: Rename to describe the action: e.g. `handleSubmit` → `saveUserProfile`, `handleClick` → `toggleSidebar`

diagnostics.message: Array index "idx" used as key — causes bugs when list is reordered or filtered
diagnostics.help: Use a stable unique identifier: `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}` — index keys break on reorder/filter

diagnostics.message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a regular prop on function components — `forwardRef` is no longer needed in React 19+. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)` for branch-aware context reads. Only enabled on projects detected as React 19+.

diagnostics.message: Avoid storing derived state. Compute "localIsOpen" directly during render, optionally with `useMemo` if it's expensive.
Avoid using props and effects as an event handler. Instead, move the handler to the parent component.

diagnostics.message: space-y-1.5 on a flex/grid parent — use gap-y-1.5 instead. Per-sibling margins phantom-gap on conditional render and don't mirror in RTL
diagnostics.help: Use `gap-*` on the flex/grid parent. `space-x-*` / `space-y-*` produce phantom gaps when a sibling is conditionally rendered, lose vertical spacing on wrapped lines, and don't mirror in RTL

diagnostics.message: useState "isMounted" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
diagnostics.help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component

diagnostics.message: useEffect re-subscribes a "handleEscape" listener every time the handler identity changes — store the handler in a ref and have the listener read `handlerRef.current()`, then drop it from the deps

diagnostics.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. diagnostics.message: Enforce a clickable non-interactive element has at least one keyboard event listener.

diagnostics.help: Either give the label a `htmlFor` attribute with the id of the associated control, or wrap the label around the control.

diagnostics.help: Either give the label a `htmlFor` attribute with the id of the associated control, or wrap the label around the control.

diagnostics.message: Non-descriptive handler name "handleChange" — name should describe what it does, not when it runs
diagnostics.help: Rename to describe the action: e.g. `handleSubmit` → `saveUserProfile`, `handleClick` → `toggleSidebar`

diagnostics.message: Array index "idx" used as key — causes bugs when list is reordered or filtered
diagnostics.help: Use a stable unique identifier: `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}` — index keys break on reorder/filter

diagnostics.message: useContext is superseded by `use()` on React 19+ — `use()` reads context conditionally inside hooks, branches, and loops; switch to `import { use } from 'react'`
diagnostics.help: Pass `ref` as a regular prop on function components — `forwardRef` is no longer needed in React 19+. Replace `useContext(X)` with `use(X)` for branch-aware context reads. Only enabled on projects detected as React 19+.

diagnostics.message: Avoid storing derived state. Compute "localIsOpen" directly during render, optionally with `useMemo` if it's expensive.
Avoid using props and effects as an event handler. Instead, move the handler to the parent component.

diagnostics.message: space-y-1.5 on a flex/grid parent — use gap-y-1.5 instead. Per-sibling margins phantom-gap on conditional render and don't mirror in RTL
diagnostics.help: Use `gap-*` on the flex/grid parent. `space-x-*` / `space-y-*` produce phantom gaps when a sibling is conditionally rendered, lose vertical spacing on wrapped lines, and don't mirror in RTL

diagnostics.message: useState "isMounted" is updated but never read in the component's return — use useRef so updates don't trigger re-renders
diagnostics.help: Replace useState with useRef when the value is only mutated and never read in render — `ref.current = ...` updates without re-rendering the component
